title: Seismic analysis and distribution of a bottom-simulating reflector (BSR) in the Chilean margin offshore of Valdivia (40° S)
journal: Journal of South American Earth Sciences
abstract: Multi-channel seismic reflection data collected offshore from Valdivia (40°S) were processed to examine structural characteristics and the presence of bottom-simulating reflectors (BSR). Seismic structure velocity was determined through 1-D forward modeling, comparing recorded seismograms with synthetic data using varying physical parameters. Results confirm the presence of gas hydrates above the BSR, with the reflector's spatial continuity appearing interrupted or irregular due to faulting. The BSR depth and velocity analysis indicate thermally controlled gas hydrate stability conditions consistent with local heat flow variations.

location.description: Offshore Valdivia at 40°S, central-southern Chilean margin

seismic_lines.name: SO161-26 — SPOC Area A trench-perpendicular line offshore Valdivia (~40°S); 227 km total, 111 km processed

data.name: Multichannel seismic reflection profiles offshore Valdivia (BSR study)
data.description: MCS reflection data processed to identify and characterize BSR and gas hydrate distribution offshore Valdivia (40°S). Velocity analysis and 1D forward modeling used to determine gas hydrate concentrations.

processing.summary: Multichannel seismic reflection data from offshore Valdivia (40°S) were processed to image the BSR and quantify gas hydrate distribution. Processing included standard MCS workflow (filtering, velocity analysis, stack, migration) followed by 1D forward modelling to derive velocity structure at BSR depth. The analysis involved comparison of recorded seismograms with synthetic waveforms to constrain the physical properties of hydrate-bearing sediments.
workflow: 1. Data acquisition (SO161-26, SPOC 2001; 132-ch 3000m streamer, 20 airguns 51.2L, 50m shots, 4ms, 14s records)
14. Depth conversion (Dix 1955 from NMO velocity model)
15. BSR 1-D forward waveform modeling (Vera et al. 1990 reflectivity method)
processing.notes: Data from SPOC SO161 cruise (2001); released by BGR (Germany) for analysis. Authors: Rodrigo at CICESE/INACH (Mexico/Chile), Vera at Universidad de Chile, González-Fernández at CICESE. Processing: 20 airguns 51.2L (3124 cu in), 3000m 132-ch streamer (first 24 at 12.5m, next 108 at 25m), 14s records at 4ms, ~50m shot interval, fold=27. Processing used Yilmaz (1987) standard workflow; post-stack Gazdag migration; depth conversion with Dix (1955). 1-D forward modeling using Vera et al. (1990) reflectivity code. Only 108-channel subset used for processing (CMP spacing 12.5m).

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Processing: Gazdag post-stack migration, Dix (1955) depth conversion, Vera et al. (1990) reflectivity forward modeling. Authors at CICESE/INACH (not SHOA as previously noted).
